steps.title: Name the implied weakness
steps.content: The finding says lobbying does little to alter whether a policy is enacted. The next sentence pushes back by saying lobbying can still shape which proposals are considered. The blank must describe lobbying as seemingly unable to produce results.

steps.title: Reject stronger or unrelated traits
steps.content: A means actively harmful, but the finding shows little effect, not backfiring. B concerns distrustful motives, which are not discussed. C concerns unfair preference, but the passage is about effectiveness, not fairness. The answer is D.
